text: Hello everyone, today I will show you the video about laparoscopic parietal duodenectomy.
As you know, the procedure involves resection of the parietal head, duodenum, gallbladder, and bile, with or without removal of the elastic angina.
The surgeon mobilizes the right omentum, lifting it upwards from the transverse colon to gain access to the surgical field.
The surgeon divides the loose attachments between the posterior gastric wall and the anterior surface of the pancreas, separating these structures.
The surgeon frees the hepatic flexure of the colon from its retroperitoneal attachment, facilitating the exposure and mobilization of the pancreatic head and duodenum.

The surgeon incises the visceral peritoneum along the inferior border of the pancreas, starting from the left of the middle colic vessel and extending toward the patient's right and inferiorly, exposing the transverse section of the middle colic vein and the superior mesenteric vein (SMV).
Inside of SMV, there are several fans that friend directly into SMV, so it is easy to enjoy the visual and magnetic.
I must stop the video here and see you later in my next video about WIPO procedure.
